definition.mission: Prevent specs regressions by making every non-trivial specs addition traceable to ADR raw intent, promoted purpose lineage, and destructive-case relations that can be imported as Nix data.
definition.responsibility: Define how specs consumes the adr repo JSONL input for specs-addition intent, purpose lineage, destructive-test candidates, and raw-to-promoted relations without copying raw ADR authority into specs.

mustFailIf: specs becomes the raw ADR source of truth
ADR relation JSONL is missing for a specs addition
promoted purpose has no raw ADR relation
destructive case has no purpose reference
example files are imported as canonical relation input
ADR input is treated as approval, merge-ready, or policy rewrite authority
mustPass: raw ADR JSONL storage path is fixed in adr
purpose lineage and destructive-case promotion paths are fixed in adr
raw-to-promoted relation JSONL path is fixed in adr
specs consumes ADR paths as Nix data input
specs does not copy raw ADR into specs issues or packages as a second authority
destructive-case records reference a purpose lineage record
relation records reference existing raw and promoted records
example/poc/usecase and example/poc/example are not treated as canonical input

adrInputContract.authorityBoundary: ADR input is rationale evidence, not approval, merge-ready status, policy rewrite authority, or implementation authorization.
adrInputContract.exampleBoundary: adr/example contains non-canonical proof/example paths under example/poc/usecase and example/poc/example; neither is canonical input
adrInputContract.importRule: Specs packages may import ADR JSONL paths as data through the adr Nix input; specs must not become the raw ADR store.
adrInputContract.promotionRule: Raw ADR records may be promoted to purpose lineage and destructive-test candidate records only through explicit relation JSONL.
adrInputContract.regressionGateRule: A specs contract addition is incomplete when its ADR relation path is missing, when a promoted purpose has no raw ADR relation, or when destructive-test candidates do not reference a purpose.
adrInputContract.srcBoundary: adr/src is lib only: core, port, adapter

rawDefinition.mission: Prevent specs regressions by making every non-trivial specs addition traceable to ADR raw intent, promoted purpose lineage, and destructive-case relations that can be imported as Nix data.

outputReviewGate.packageOutputRule: officialOutput is the canonical Nix package output for the worker task.

artifact existence or JSONL parse success is treated as issue closure
gate requires complete legacy backfill for untouched packages

destructiveCaseCoverage.expectedDefense: Each triggered specs growth proposal must classify which cases apply, either satisfy the expected defense or explicitly park the case as blocker/deferred debt.

forbiddenPaths: bulk moving reports or generated artifacts
rewriting existing ADR or issue history
global dashboard or registry as source of truth

growthIntegrityContract.closureRule: Issue closure cannot be inferred from artifact existence, parse success, generated pass text, or one reviewer reply. Closure requires closeCriteria evidence and destructive-case disposition.
growthIntegrityContract.duplicateWorkRule: If two open issue records touch the same package or authority surface, the later issue must declare dependsOn, conflictsWith, supersedes, or ownerTransfer before implementation starts.
growthIntegrityContract.generatedEvidenceRule: Generated reports and run summaries can support review but cannot define package truth, ADR truth, close criteria, or approval state.
normativeAuthorityExamples: accepted ADR JSONL records when imported by pinned relation input
growthIntegrityContract.schemaEvolutionRule: New JSONL kind/schema records require compatibility notes, unsupported-schema behavior, and downgrade/correction path before specs consumers treat them as accepted.

issue touching existing package contract or authority surface
new JSONL kind or schema version consumed by specs
proposal citing generated report, review prose, or bundle manifest as evidence
package family or broad wrapper introduced from precedent

definition.mission: Keep performance testing language-neutral by measuring API, CLI, batch, browser, build-time, file-size, and custom benchmark surfaces from the outside, then using Bencher as the ledger, comparison, threshold, alert, and CI fail gate.

mustFailIf: U1 is claimed only by preserving upstream spec17 baseline
Bencher/performance package paths are absent from final zip
Bencher CLI release source, hash, and license are silently treated as final
host-native nix flake check is claimed without being run

packages/bencher-bench-gate/contracts/bmf-normalization.v1.json exists
packages/bencher-bench-gate/contracts/threshold-contract.v1.json exists

case-tdd bencher-bench-gate passes
u1_bencher_paths_present passes

blockedWhen: BMF normalization contract path is missing

runner output cannot be normalized to BMF JSON

host-native nix flake check is claimed without being run

mustFailIf: canonical branch is used as ordinary work surface

direct canonical commit repair discards the unexpected head without bundle or quarantine ref
hook presence is treated as proof of governance correctness
policy prose is the only source for package meaning
mustPass: package contract resolves repoId, repoPlacement, repoSourceUri, outputs, checks, allowed paths, forbidden paths, return contract, and manifest contract
attach-only check requires candidate branch or worktree evidence
no-drift check treats current canonical head mismatch as stale-review, not merge approval
direct canonical commit detection excludes approved localizer merge evidence
quarantine contract preserves previous approved head and unexpected current head before reset
local hooks and remote bare hooks are optional enforcement surfaces, not the only authority
worker can return RUN_REPORT.md, canonical-branch-guard-report.json, gate-log.md, and RESIDUAL_RISKS.md

blockedWhen: candidate branch or worktree is missing
current canonical head cannot be read
quarantine output cannot preserve the unexpected head

evidence: git merge-tree or equivalent no-conflict evidence
quarantine ref or bundle evidence when direct commit is found
guarantees: canonical branch attach-only boundary is checkable
merge-review-pass is stale if current canonical head drifted from reviewed base
direct canonical commits are detected separately from approved localizer merges
quarantine preserves refs before destructive repair
local and remote bare hooks remain enforcement aids, not semantic approval

definition.responsibility: Implement caselaw: a local-first package that consumes normalized case, claim, evidence, and optional preaccident fixtures, then produces fingerprints, contrast sets, candidate/tested law records, exportable gate specs, and regression cases.

mustFailIf: raw papers, Project discussions, boards, logs, or agent logs are directly extracted by caselaw
board IR, board renderer state, board protocol, or UI view state is owned by caselaw
event truth, status view, status read model, issue ledger, expectation ledger, source artifact ledger, or incident ledger is owned by caselaw
incident runtime, active policy truth, active law runtime, or gate execution runtime is owned by caselaw
candidate law becomes active law without owner adoption
gateSpecContract is treated as an executable gate runtime
lawRegistry is treated as active policy truth
claim text is treated as truth without evidence
claimd package output or command alias is exposed
feature implementation code is placed in repos/specs
mustPass: packages.<system>.caselaw exposes the CLI
checks.<system>.caselaw runs unit and CLI smoke tests
caselaw rejects malformed case, claim, and evidence records
caselaw separates proposed claims, candidate laws, tested laws, and active/adopted governance
caselaw requires evidence refs before law adoption metadata is accepted
caselaw fingerprints equivalent cases deterministically
caselaw can consume preaccident fixture outputs as soft inputs
caselaw can export gate specs without executing them
caselaw can produce a machine-readable result manifest

blockedWhen: worker attempts to implement caselaw inside repos/specs

guarantees: normalized input schemas are enforced
forbidden ownership surfaces are rejected
candidate/tested law records are separated from active policy truth
exportable gate specs are separated from gate execution
case fingerprints and regression outputs are reproducible

compatibility wrappers inside the cdp package only
definition.responsibility: Expose one Nix package output for the CDP app while delegating browser and CDP work to Kuri and preserving mjs assets as the app boundary.

blockedWhenMissing: quickjs-ng runtime is unavailable for final package checks
Kuri source, binary, hash, or license is unavailable
Chrome/Chromium live CDP endpoint is unavailable for live integration checks

failureIsBlockedWhen: Kuri release or source hash is missing
a command still depends on flakes-owned cdp-bridge
the package creates multiple new CDP Nix outputs instead of one cdp output
ChatGPT adapter starts or stops the server without explicit cdp serve
mjs files are bulk-deleted instead of classified by boundary role
mjs meaning modules are moved into Kuri
old names remain as package outputs instead of aliases or tombstones
a known risk is left without mitigation, check, blocked state, or deferred note
requiredChecks: naming contract declares final cdp name, old-to-new map, and alias retirement rules
risk mitigation contract converts critical objections into checks or deferred states
cdp health reports Kuri reachability or a clear KURI_NOT_RUNNING error
grep proves no built mjs command calls cdp-bridge
grep proves no built mjs command calls Chrome /json endpoints
grep proves no built mjs command opens a raw CDP WebSocket
mjs asset manifest classifies each kept, ported, rewritten, and retired mjs file
rename ledger proves old command and package names map to canonical cdp names or tombstones
old command names still resolve from the cdp package when kept as compatibility wrappers
warm read-thread, search, inventory, and artifact paths report stats.cdp.* == 0
result manifest lists deleted CDP transport files, rewritten mjs files, kept mjs assets, changed Nix outputs, final names, and mitigated risks

blockedWhen: repo snapshot for flakes is missing
Kuri source, hash, or license is missing
quickjs-ng runtime is missing for final checks
Chrome/Chromium live CDP is missing for live integration checks
a legacy CDP output would be produced
raw CDP bridge, Chrome /json, or WebSocket transport remains in built mjs
mjs assets are bulk-deleted without boundary classification
nixosModules.* requirement is unresolved without review gate

caseScope.rule: This file may use case terms because it is the package case spec. The same terms must not appear in core policy.

package-contracts.purpose: Use registered extension mechanisms so CDP facts do not leak into core policy.

purpose-toolchain.purpose: Use pinned tool and release policy before adopting Kuri, Zig, Chromium, QuickJS-ng, or mjs assets.

repository-governance.purpose: Use repository ownership and duplicate-audit rules to prove old CDP transport was removed while mjs boundary assets were either preserved, ported, or explicitly retired.

layering: Kuri owns Chrome launch, attach, tabs, snapshots, refs, actions, upload, cookies, storage, and raw CDP.
The cdp package owns command routing, stable stdout JSON, and compatibility names.
The mjs assets own ChatGPT selectors, flow meaning, command behavior, download resolve/fetch policy, and IR.
No layer may duplicate another layer's transport or state.

mjsBoundary.currentRole: app boundary between user commands and Kuri HTTP
deleteOnlyWhen: the command has an equal cdp package replacement
the migration report records the replacement path
tests prove no user-facing behavior was lost
mjsBoundary.futureRole: candidate embedded assets for a future Zig host; this is recorded but not implemented now
keepOrPort: parts/cdp/chromium-cdp-*.mjs compatibility wrappers when still useful
mustNotDeleteAsGroup: legacy command wrappers before compatibility is proven unnecessary
rewriteFirst: parts/cdp/chromium-cdp.lib.mjs remove cdp-bridge calls and delegate to Kuri HTTP client
parts/cdp/connect.mjs remove raw WebSocket connect or replace with Kuri HTTP client
parts/cdp/cdp-open.mjs call Kuri tab/open endpoint instead of Chrome /json endpoints
parts/cdp/chromium-cdp-status.mjs call cdp health or Kuri /health
mjsBoundary.rule: mjs is the boundary asset. Keep or port meaning modules; only remove transport-only modules after a Kuri HTTP replacement exists.
mjsBoundary.runtime: QuickJS-ng-compatible mjs asset set inside packages.<system>.cdp

implementationContract.serverRule: Kuri is the only browser/CDP server. cdp serve may start Kuri only when explicitly invoked. All other cdp commands only health-check and use an existing Kuri base URL.
implementationContract.singlePackageRule: Build one cdp package. Put compatibility command names inside that package instead of adding many Nix package outputs.
implementationContract.upstreamRule: Kuri is an upstream input and Nix module. Treat Kuri as the only browser/CDP backend, not as app-specific ChatGPT logic.

localExceptionProposals.activateOnlyIf: A named ChatGPT operation has no Kuri endpoint and the call is recorded in an evaluate ledger.
localExceptionProposals.expiresWhen: The needed Kuri endpoint exists or the evaluate call is replaced by snapshot/action/upload/storage APIs.
localExceptionProposals.promotionPath: package-contracts.backendAdapterEscapeHatch if another backend replacement needs the same pattern.
localExceptionProposals.reason: A narrow evaluate window may let the adapter move to Kuri without reintroducing raw CDP WebSocket transport.
localExceptionProposals.recommendation: Add only if a missing Kuri HTTP endpoint blocks migration; otherwise keep it out.

localExceptionProposals.reason: Compatibility wrappers are expected migration data, not a rule exception.
localExceptionProposals.recommendation: Keep this in naming-ledger alias retire conditions, not as a local exception.

localExceptionProposals.reason: The QuickJS-ng embedded Zig host idea is useful, but activating it now would merge a future architecture into the current migration.
localExceptionProposals.recommendation: Keep this as a deferred future candidate, not as a local exception.

localExceptionRules.expiresWhen: Kuri source, hash, license, module contract, and cdp package build are locked, and no legacy transport import remains.
localExceptionRules.promotionPath: package-contracts.backendReplacementProfile if another package performs the same backend replacement.
localExceptionRules.reason: The migration would otherwise keep two CDP backends alive.

localExceptionRules.expiresWhen: Every mjs file is classified as keep, port, rewrite, or retire, and transport-only files are rewritten or retired.
localExceptionRules.promotionPath: package-contracts.boundaryAssetManifest if another package protects script assets during backend replacement.
localExceptionRules.reason: The valuable asset is ChatGPT operation meaning in mjs; only raw CDP transport should disappear.

delete: Nix outputs named cdp-bridge, chromium-cdp-wsurl, chromium-cdp-tools

deletionLedger.flakesCatalogUse: migration-only; not upstream, not runtime dependency, not production package source
deletionLedger.purpose: Prove deletion of legacy transport without erasing mjs app boundary assets.

keep: mjs boundary assets after they call Kuri HTTP
ChatGPT adapter meaning files after they call Kuri HTTP
IR schema for threads, thread detail, search, inventory, artifacts, and _cdp stats
Legacy command names as wrappers inside packages.<system>.cdp only
mustNotMoveIntoKuri: Policy values for ChatGPT polling and artifact download
quarantine: parts/chromedevtoolprotocol.zig as future host candidate only
replaceWith: one flake package output named cdp
mjs ChatGPT boundary modules that depend on the client module
compatibility wrappers only where they reduce migration cost
rewriteOrRetire: any chromium-cdp-*.mjs wrapper that only exists to call removed transport

alias has a migration ledger entry

namingContract.tombstoneRule: After deletion, old names may appear only in cdp-migration-ledger or cdp-deletion-report.

packageContents: QuickJS-ng-compatible runtime contract for mjs assets

exports.contract: packages.<system>.cdp is the single official CDP app output. It contains the runnable cdp command set and may expose legacy command names inside bin, but no separate cdp-bridge package.
exports.name: cdpPackage

exports.contract: Client commands talk to Kuri HTTP only. They must not call Chrome /json endpoints, must not open raw CDP WebSockets, and must not compile flakes-owned CDP bridge code.
exports.name: kuriHttpClient

exports.contract: The mjs files are the app boundary asset for ChatGPT meaning, selectors, command behavior, IR, and compatibility. They are not bulk-delete targets. Transport-only mjs code must be rewritten to Kuri HTTP or deleted only after its command has a replacement.
exports.name: mjsBoundaryAssets

exports.contract: ChatGPT logic owns selectors, navigation meaning, session probe, input helper, artifact locator, and IR. It does not own browser lifecycle or CDP transport.
exports.name: chatgptAdapter

exports.contract: Warm paths read IR first. Fresh thread, search, inventory, and artifact IR must prove zero live browser calls through stats.cdp.* == 0.
exports.name: lowAccessIr

exports.contract: Result must list removed CDP transport files, rewritten mjs boundary files, kept mjs assets, replaced commands, and the checks proving no raw CDP transport remains in the app layer.
exports.name: migrationReport

exports.contract: The final refactored surface is named cdp. All old chromium-cdp command names are aliases inside the cdp package only, with explicit retireWhen conditions. No old name may remain as a Nix output or runtime import.
exports.name: finalNaming

exports.contract: Known critical objections must be converted into checks, retire conditions, blocked states, or deferred notes. The result may not leave open critique items without a mitigation.
exports.name: riskMitigation

rawDefinition.responsibility: Expose one Nix package output for the CDP app while delegating browser and CDP work to Kuri and preserving mjs assets as the app boundary.

mitigations.mitigation: Keep old command names only as wrappers inside cdp, each mapped to a canonical cdp command and retireWhen condition.
mitigations.risk: Old chromium-cdp names stay forever and keep the old mental model alive.

mitigations.mitigation: Require mjs-boundary-asset-manifest classification: kept, ported, rewritten, or retired.
mitigations.risk: mjs assets are accidentally bulk-deleted even though they are the ChatGPT boundary.

mitigations.mitigation: Forbid raw CDP transport in built mjs and require Kuri HTTP client usage.
mitigations.risk: Transport logic returns through connect.mjs, Chrome /json endpoints, or raw WebSocket usage.

mitigations.mitigation: Allow server start only through cdp serve; all other commands perform health check and fail clearly when Kuri is missing.
mitigations.risk: Kuri server management leaks into normal app commands.

mitigations.mitigation: Mark it as quarantined future-host candidate only and forbid runtime imports.
mitigations.risk: chromedevtoolprotocol.zig is reused as a second runtime backend.

mitigations.mitigation: Reserve the name cdp-zig-host and keep futureZigHostStatus as deferred, with no runtime dependency.
mitigations.risk: The future QuickJS-ng Zig host idea bloats the current migration.

mitigations.mitigation: Keep missing Kuri release data as blocked releaseNeed until spec records source, hash, and license.
mitigations.risk: Kuri adoption blocks final because source, hash, or license is missing.

profileSchema.authorityRule: A profile is capability data. It is not command authority, transport proof, semantic approval, completion approval, impl-review-pass, merge-review-pass, localizer approval, merge, push, or cleanup authority.
profileSchema.freshnessRule: If now is after expiresAt or evidenceRefs are missing, consumers must treat the profile as stale-profile and request refresh before relying on it for handoff construction.

projectSourceFileLimit.consumptionRule: Consumers must read the effective project file budget from this profile, not hard-code 40 everywhere. If the plan is unknown, default to the lowest verified project file budget in this profile or require operator confirmation before Project Source upload.
projectSourceFileLimit.nonAuthorityRule: File visibility and project memory are capability facts only; they do not prove approval, review pass, merge readiness, or policy compliance.
projectSourceFileLimit.sourcePackRule: Project Source source-pack generation should target <= 32 files and reserve at least 8 files as buffer when the effective budget is 40.

mustFailIf: a new domain requires a hard-coded branch in kernel core
domain-specific simulator logic is added under the kernel source
projection output is treated as authoritative source of truth
zip import evidence is treated as ongoing canonical implementation source
src imports from examples or treats example/poc/example code as library truth
required checks depend on live hosted model behavior or network access
ADR input relation is missing for promoted purpose or destructive-test intent
mustPass: package contract is discoverable from specs packages catalog
target implementation exposes packages.<system>.domain-kernel
target implementation exposes checks.<system>.domain-kernel or equivalent package-backed gates
pytest or equivalent unit suite passes
example runner proves all Tier-S examples without external services
kernel boundary check reports no domain-specific branches in core

implementationContract.canonicalStateRule: The kernel may hold a working state cache for facilitation, but existing Board or JSONL authorities remain canonical when present.
implementationContract.externalWorkerRule: The kernel prepares context and consumes worker results; it must not decide task semantics by hidden internal LLM calls.
implementationContract.threadFsmBoundary: ops-thread-fsm owns thread state classification, sleep/readback/retry flow, review states, merge states, and approval semantics. facilitator-kernel may consume those states as input but must not redefine them.

mainlineRailContract.resumeRule: After recording a non-blocking side issue, the loop restates currentCheckpointId and returns nextAction to the mainline before proposing policy changes.

rawDefinition.mission: Carry the useful Hermes concepts into a backend kernel without adopting the Hermes app, internal LLM loop, chat UI, TUI, or duplicated canonical state.

outputReviewGate.appOutputRule: Flake application outputs and slash-form application package outputs are forbidden. Package work must expose packages.<system> outputs only.
outputReviewGate.devShellOutputRule: devShells are local environment conveniences, not feat package work outputs.
outputReviewGate.exceptionRule: Only a specs-declared package-specific outputReviewGate exception may relax forbidden top-level outputs.
outputReviewGate.nixosModulesRule: If requiredOutputs contains nixosModules.*, generated inputs must mark module output review as required unless the flake exposes the module output.
outputReviewGate.packageOutputRule: officialOutput is the canonical Nix package output for the worker task.

packageContents: schemas for envelope, context packet, worker result, gate decision, store event, and projection
planned lifecycle note: required outputs and checks are activation targets until implementation-ready review
rawDefinition.plannedReason: The source Project defines the package intent and minimum DoD, but implementation ownership and schemas still need specs review before feat work starts.

exports.contract: Accepts an envelope, builds a context packet, invokes an external worker adapter, gates the result, appends store events, and emits a projection.
exports.name: facilitationLoop

exports.contract: Caches facilitation state for the current run and references external canonical records without replacing them.
exports.name: workingStateCache

exports.contract: Records pass, retry, blocked, or needs-parent decisions with evidence and next action.
exports.name: gateDecision

exports.contract: Provides a view of current facilitation state while remaining non-authoritative.
exports.name: projection

exports.contract: Requires one machine-readable worker.result.v1 line from external workers; absence or parse failure becomes a record, not a human reminder.
exports.name: workerResultJsonl

exports.contract: Carries mainlineGoal, currentCheckpointId, sideIssue parking, blocker reason codes, and resume rule through the facilitation loop.
exports.name: mainlineRail

exports.contract: Separates threadLane from threadFunction so index/governance remain snapshot/report lanes while impl/merge work/review keep existing functions.
exports.name: threadLaneAuthority

rawDefinition.responsibility: Define a single headless backend package that coordinates external agents through a small facilitation loop, working state cache, gate, event log, and projection surface.

threadLaneAuthorityContract.governanceRule: Governance may report policy findings, risks, and recommendations; it must not rewrite policy, assign implementation, approve, merge, push, or localize inside the mission.
threadLaneAuthorityContract.indexRule: Index may snapshot observed records and report missing/invalid/stale/conflict state; it must not judge validity, approve, or declare merge-ready.

workerResultJsonlContract.claimEvidenceRule: Claims without evidenceRef, sourceRecordId, artifact hash, gate log, or review verdict stay unverified in projections.
workerResultJsonlContract.laneFunctionRule: threadFunction is null for index and governance; otherwise it must match one of the four existing work/review functions.
workerResultJsonlContract.noCliRule: The facilitation contract requires the JSONL return line or artifact, not a mission-control CLI invocation by the worker.
workerResultJsonlContract.purpose: Make the external worker return path machine-readable without requiring the worker to run a CLI.
workerResultJsonlContract.trustBoundary: worker.result.v1 is a low-trust claim envelope. It is not approval, merge-ready, completion, or canonical adoption.

oracle truth is hard-coded into reusable core

target implementation exposes packages.<system>.failed-assumption-ledger
target implementation exposes checks.<system>.failed-assumption-ledger or equivalent package-backed gates
pytest or equivalent unit suite passes
policy proof reproduces expected observed/diff/learning outputs
contribution external-factor proof reproduces expected pass/fail matrix
JSONL ledger lint rejects if then control-flow semantics
projection adapters preserve ledger truth and mark projections as derived
host_obedience_e2e is present only as Tier-H adoption evidence if used
ADR JSONL input contract records relation paths when promoted purpose or destructive-test intent is claimed
source repo manifest binds to spec.packages.failed-assumption-ledger.v1
source layout check proves src as lib and example/poc/example as non-authority usecase evidence

nonPromotionRules: raw ADR JSONL is source rationale, not specs package truth
promotion relation JSONL is input evidence, not merge approval
promoted objective JSONL is not measured outcome
promoted destructive-test JSONL is a test input candidate, not a passing test result
Nix input presence is not semantic approval
ADR repo source update is not localizer authorization

adrRepoOwns: raw ADR JSONL for specs-addition intent
relations from raw ADR to promoted objective/destructive-test JSONL

blockedWhen: implementation manifest does not claim spec.packages.failed-assumption-ledger.v1
policy proof expected matrix is missing
contribution proof expected matrix is missing
required checks are only prose claims

catalogDecision.immediateEffect: Package catalog entry exists; target implementation still proves conformance through its own repo bundle.
catalogDecision.reason: The package closes the break-case loop from observed failure to failed assumption, lifecycle, regression, guard, and policy candidate. Leaving it outside specs would keep model strengthening and promotion governance outside mechanical checks.
rejectedAlternatives: keep as unregistered bare repo
embed zip payload under specs
treat break cases as plain logs
fold promotion policy into domain-kernel
make live hosted model obedience a Tier-S required check

guarantees: failed assumptions are represented as evidence records
JSONL remains a ledger rather than an execution language
policy proof and contribution proof match expected outputs
optional external implementations are adapters, not core truth
Tier-S checks are deterministic and offline-capable
source repo implementation binds to this package contract
src/example dependency direction is explicit and checkable

contractizationTrigger.boundary: Risk/fear is a trigger to record evidence and constraints, not authority. Contractization is not verified implementation, approval, localizer authorization, production verification, or measured retained-earnings impact.
contractizationTrigger.minimalityRationale: A specs package contract is the narrowest stable-responsibility surface: it defines ledger boundaries, deterministic checks, Tier-H separation, lifecycle, and non-claims without importing implementation payloads or adding purpose-specific packages.

keep as unregistered bare repo
treat break cases as plain logs
fold promotion policy into domain-kernel
make live hosted model obedience a Tier-S required check
create an objective-specific package instead of recording objective fit as data/profile/ledger rows

guard, regression, and policy-promotion candidates need evidence-shaped lifecycle records

hosted model evidence may be accidentally promoted into deterministic required gates

definition.responsibility: Define the language-agnostic functional-core governance contract and bind its executable static gate to the ops feat repo without making ADR, examples, generated reports, or policy prose into implementation authority.

sourceAdr.boundary: ADR supplies intent and destructive-case evidence only; specs defines the package contract; ops implements the gate.

mustFailIf: a core file reads hidden clock/random/env/filesystem/network/database/browser-storage/subprocess/global mutable state
a core file imports or depends on adapter/runtime code
a checker scans all repo files and fails examples by default
ADR raw records are treated as approval, merge readiness, or implementation authority
a style mandate such as monads, currying, FP libraries, recursion-only, loop ban, or class ban is introduced
specs contains executable checker implementation
mustPass: specs defines spec.packages.functional-core-governance-gate.v1 as contract-only
ops/spec/implements.json declares spec.packages.functional-core-governance-gate.v1
ops package source uses src/ as library and bin/ as thin wrapper
valid fixture manifest passes with ok=true and semanticsProfile=functional-core-governance-v1
hidden-effect fixture fails with diagnostic kind hidden-effect-in-core
example/poc/example is not treated as canonical input unless explicitly passed
ADR relation records exist for the raw ADR, promoted purpose, and destructive cases

blockedWhen: manifest does not explicitly list core files
declared core file contains hidden effect tokens
declared core file imports adapter/runtime code
implementation package does not declare spec.packages.functional-core-governance-gate.v1 in ops-main/spec/implements.json

guarantees: specs remains contract-only
implementation contract points to ops
functional-core classification is explicit, not repo-wide by default
hidden effects in declared core files are hard failures
language-specific style mandates are forbidden
ADR records remain intent/evidence input only

dependencyUse.adr-jsonl-intent-input: functional-core ADR raw/purpose/destructive-case/relation records are evidence input, not approval authority
dependencyUse.issue-ledger-unified-kernel: workspace issue JSONL audit confirms new ADR/specs/ops issues remain issue.record.v1
dependencyUse.prove-feat: merge-time package contract and implementation evidence gate

forbiddenPaths: style-mandate rules unrelated to hidden effects, explicit inputs, effects-as-data, or dependency direction

coreMust: compute deterministic outputs from explicit inputs
return effects as data for adapters/runtimes to execute
receive clock/random/env/filesystem/network/database/browser-storage/subprocess capabilities explicitly as values when needed
keep dependency direction adapter/runtime -> core, never core -> adapter/runtime
coreMustNot: read hidden wall-clock time, randomness, environment, filesystem, network, database, browser storage, subprocesses, or global mutable state
execute returned effects inside the functional core
turn examples, tests, or generated reports into authority
functionalCorePolicy.scope: only packages, modules, or files explicitly classified as functional-core by a manifest or future specs package contract

prohibitedFeatClaims: all code in the repository is functional-core by default
ADR records approve or merge the gate
style mandates such as monads, currying, recursion-only, loop bans, or class bans are required
examples, generated reports, or PoC data are canonical input unless explicitly passed as fixtures
requiredFeatCapabilities: evaluate only manifest-declared core files by default
fail hidden effect tokens in declared core files
fail adapter/runtime dependency tokens in declared core files
keep source-reading and CLI behavior in adapter/runtime code, not the pure evaluation core

rawDefinition.mission: Prevent hidden effects, mutable caller-owned state, and adapter/core dependency inversion from entering packages that are explicitly classified as functional-core, while rejecting language-specific FP style mandates.

exports.contract: This package is a Windows host + NixOS WSL storage hygiene contract. It is not a generic Linux cleanup package and not a specs-hosted implementation.
exports.name: scope

exports.contract: Daily operation has exactly three normal entrypoints: analyze, tmp-dry-run, and tmp-apply with an explicit plan.
exports.name: dailyEntrypoints

exports.contract: tmp-apply must consume a dry-run plan artifact. Recomputing candidates at apply time is not sufficient acceptance.
exports.name: cleanupPlan

exports.contract: A candidate must be under NixOS /tmp, match a configured pattern, be outside keep-list, be outside active process paths, be older than 72 hours, and have known metadata.
exports.name: deleteCandidateRule

exports.contract: keep-listed, recent, active, unknown, pattern-mismatch, persistent-login-profile, and non-/tmp items must not be deleted.
exports.name: deleteRefusalRule

exports.contract: Reports and plans must not print cookies, tokens, browser storage contents, credential material, prompt bodies, or full process argument inventories.
exports.name: secretRedaction

exports.contract: Login state is a persistent profile/snapshot concern. Disposable runtime copies may live in /tmp and be cleaned only by the plan/apply rules.
exports.name: profileStateBoundary

exports.contract: CDP profile placement is not route proof. Target Project URL access, Project Source upload, thread creation, and delayed readback remain owned by ops-cdp-core.
exports.name: cdpRouteBoundary

exports.contract: WSL VHDX compaction, hiberfil changes, and login profile promotion are separate operations and must not be hidden inside /tmp cleanup.
exports.name: separateOperations

exports.contract: Candidate safety conditions are backed by positive and negative fixtures under contracts/fixtures and checked by check-tmp-cleanup-plan-fixtures.sh.
exports.name: schemaFixtures

rawDefinition.responsibility: Define the acceptance contract for Windows-hosted NixOS WSL temporary storage cleanup without importing host-local implementation scripts into specs.

risks.mitigation: Keep lifecycle planned and scope contract explicit until host-config owns a real implementation and gates.
risks.risk: A Windows/NixOS WSL cleanup tool is mistaken for a portable specs package.

risks.check: cleanupSafetyContract.mustNotDelete includes persistent login profile or published snapshot.
risks.mitigation: Persistent profiles are outside /tmp cleanup and profile lifecycle remains owned by ops-cdp-core/host profile contracts.
risks.risk: A runtime cleanup deletes persistent login state or a published snapshot.

risks.check: cleanupSafetyContract.planRequired and applyMustUsePlan are true.
risks.mitigation: tmp-apply must require a plan artifact and re-check only refusal conditions.
risks.risk: Apply recomputes candidates that differ from the human-reviewed plan.

risks.check: forbiddenReportFields contains cookie, token, storage, credential, and process args entries.
risks.mitigation: Report contract forbids secret fields and process argument inventories.
risks.risk: Reports reveal cookies, tokens, storage contents, or credentials.

definition.responsibility: Own the adapter-neutral interaction history IR, deterministic id rules, reducer contracts, context-pack contract, and neutral view-input boundary for ChatGPT, Codex, CDP readback, and future conversation sources.

mustFailIf: a source adapter is required to depend on another source adapter
history status is treated as actor lifecycle truth
transcript prose, CDP readback, or terminal output is treated as approval or merge-ready
viewer UI or renderer implementation enters the core package
source-specific parsing rules become core reducer semantics
mustPass: core contract defines adapter-neutral IR and deterministic id requirements
core contract defines reducer outputs shared by all adapters
core contract names chatgpt-history-foundation only as current monolith implementation evidence, not as an adapter dependency target
core contract separates history status labels from actor lifecycle status and approval authority
adapter boundary forbids adapter-to-adapter dependencies

adapterMust: preserve source_kind and safe source identity
emit blocker records for unsupported shapes
preserve sensitivity metadata for tool, terminal, and artifact payloads
adapterMustNot: treat readback or transcript prose as semantic approval
invoke browser or CDP transport unless the adapter package explicitly owns that source transport
generate viewer UI or renderer implementation

blockedWhen: adapter-neutral IR cannot be stated without source-specific parser rules
history status and actor lifecycle status are conflated

guarantees: records.jsonl validates against the shared core model
inventory, status, context-pack, and neutral SDUI input are generated from adapter-neutral records
viewer UI and SDUI renderer implementation are not generated

migrationBridge.compatibilityRule: No adapter may depend on another adapter. During migration, adapters may cite chatgpt-history-foundation as implementation evidence, but their package dependency target is interaction-history-core.

targetSplit: cdp-chatgpt-history-adapter or ops-cdp-core owns live CDP readback source mapping

definition.responsibility: Define the unified repo-local issue ledger kernel contract used by specs, policy, ops, and any ADR-adjacent repo without moving issue authority into examples, generated reports, or policy prose.

mustFailIf: any discovered issues/*.jsonl row is not issue.record.v1
status is outside the issue-ledger-contracts vocabulary
recordType and status disagree
a non-v1 ADR/spec input file is placed under issues/*.jsonl
example/poc/example is scanned as canonical issue authority
generated output is marked as authority
reviewQuestions: Are all validation semantics in src/ library code, with bin/ only acting as a wrapper?
Does workspace audit fail the previously observed specs non-v1 JSONL contamination?
Does workspace audit exclude adr/example/poc/example unless explicit fixture paths are passed?
Can policy and ops validators be reduced to compatibility wrappers around the same semantics profile?

guarantees: specs remains contract-only
implementation contract points to ops
src boundary is lib-only
example boundary is poc/example-only
non-v1 issue namespace contamination is a hard failure
generated and example artifacts are not issue authority

principles.rule: specs owns contracts, vocabulary, and acceptance conditions only; specs must not contain the executable validator implementation.
non-specs repo behavior is implemented as a feat package with spec/implements.json and prove-feat evidence; policy may route or gate but must not define a second validator semantics.
canonical issue truth is only issues/*.jsonl lines of kind issue.record.v1; generated views, RUN_REPORT, ADR rationale, examples, and artifacts are evidence or projections, not issue authority.
a pass must name the semantics profile and fail on non-v1 records under issues/*.jsonl, missing required fields, invalid status/recordType pairs, and known cross-issue/non-monotonic supersedes links.

prohibitedFeatClaims: policy shell validator and ops fsck have independent meanings
ADR raw JSONL or issue.note.v1 under issues/*.jsonl is acceptable canonical issue truth
generated markdown, HTML, artifacts, or example fixtures are issue authority
workspace audit pass proves merge approval, production correctness, or complete-approved
requiredFeatCapabilities: emit machine-readable JSON summary with semanticsProfile
exclude example/poc/example from default authority discovery

boardGuideMessageRouteContract.antiGoodhartRule: Recommendation quality is evaluated by downstream gate outcomes, rework rate, safety incidents, and operator-load reduction, not by message volume or speed alone.
boardGuideMessageRouteContract.promptDraftRule: Suggested text must be safe to copy but is still a draft. Sending it creates a new event only after human/reviewer/authorized gate acceptance.

boardLayerAuthorityContract.authorityEscalationRule: No projection or guide output may be promoted to approval, merge-ready, send authorization, policy rewrite, or canonical mutation without a distinct accepted gate event from an authorized actor/role.
boardLayerAuthorityContract.evidenceRule: Past-result guidance may cite prior accepted events and outcomes, but prior success is recommendation evidence, not permission to repeat an action.

layers.name: Board Projection
layers.rule: Owns derived views such as inboxes, unavailable intents, safe next intents, and status tables; derived views are not truth beyond accepted event/state inputs.

layers.name: Board Guide
layers.rule: Owns suggested message routes, prompt drafts, resume text, and handoff drafts; guide output must carry suggested/proposal wording and requires a separate gate before send or assignment.

exports.contract: Board Ledger canonicalizes accepted events; Board FSM canonicalizes state transitions; Board Projection exposes views; Board Guide proposes next messages or routes only. Projection and Guide outputs are not approvals, sends, merges, or policy rewrites.

exports.contract: Future workflow/FSM and past-result guidance may draft suggested messages, resume prompts, retry prompts, handoff text, and inbox write templates, but every outbound send, assignment, approval, merge, or policy change still requires a separate authorized gate event.
exports.name: Board Guide message route engine

definition.mission: Make worker state auditable without editing generated status files: canonical events.jsonl is the only source of truth, while core status JSON, state.tsv, and status.md are deterministic generated views.
definition.responsibility: Provide the small Go agent-events CLI that validates canonical agent event JSONL, reduces it to backend core status, and preserves the legacy node/status/link/memo event ledger.

mustFailIf: events.jsonl is not the only source of truth
state.json is treated as required or canonical
node.created requires or accepts human-provided --id
seq, sequence, actor, or threadUrl are accepted in persisted events
state.tsv or status.md contain removed fields
canonical event payload exposes unredacted secret fields
freshness changes lifecycle status to stale
safe_next emits executable command strings
untrusted or inferred event creates terminal truth
ops-agent-events package/check output is missing
mustPass: go test ./... passes for packages/ops-agent-events
agent-events append-node allocates 0000 then 0001 without --id
agent-events validate rejects forbidden keys, id gaps, duplicate ids, forward parents, unknown targets, and malformed links
agent-events build writes state.tsv with exactly eight required columns
agent-events build writes status.md with only id/title/memo columns
agent-events validate-agent rejects malformed canonical agent-event-v1 JSONL
agent-events reduce-agent emits core status JSON with lifecycle status, freshness, safe_next, and display-safe diagnostic
ops-agent-events.status.v1 validates or normalizes through agent-status-core-contract
agent-events reduce-agent ignores untrusted terminal events as terminal truth
agent-events reduce-agent keeps stale as freshness overlay, not lifecycle status
packages.<system>.ops-agent-events exposes the Go CLI
checks.<system>.ops-agent-events runs Go tests and lightweight generated-output checks

blockedWhen: Go toolchain is unavailable for final check

guarantees: agent-events Go tests pass
generated state.tsv/status.md stay deterministic
canonical status.json stays deterministic
forbidden event fields are rejected
untrusted terminal truth is not accepted

agent-status-core-contract.purpose: Produce status output compatible with the shared core status contract and protocol-bound vocabulary without leaking reducer authority into read/view packages.

package-contracts.purpose: Keep the package-local event schema and review gates out of spec core policy while still making the ops-agent-events output discoverable.

implementationContract.statusOutputContract: ops-agent-events.status.v1 implements the producer side of agent-status-core-contract compatibility.
validationRules: malformed JSONL is rejected
unknown event type is rejected
forbidden persisted keys are rejected
canonical agent-event-v1 lines require schema_version, event_id, event_time, observed_time, source, subject, event_type, payload, and sensitivity
canonical identity is subject.agent_id plus subject.run_id
freshness is an overlay and stale is not a lifecycle status
terminal lifecycle truth requires declared source authority
safe-next is semantic advice and not execution or authorization
payload is not display-safe by default and secret-bearing payload fields require redaction marker
node ids start at 0000 and increase without gaps or duplicates
parentId is null only for root and otherwise points to a previously created node
target events reference existing nodes
typed link prefix is recognized
node.created kind and title are non-blank

exports.contract: node.created allocates flat global zero-padded decimal ids beginning at 0000. The CLI allocates by reading and validating existing events; append-node has no id flag.

exports.contract: link.assigned preserves chatgpt:, subagent:, ssh:, file:, and github: links exactly; multiple links render in event order separated by a pipe delimiter.
exports.name: typedLinks

exports.contract: agent-event-v1 JSONL envelope with source, subject, event_type, payload, and sensitivity fields.
exports.name: canonicalAgentEventSchema

exports.contract: agent-events validate-agent --events validates canonical agent-event-v1 JSONL without reducing status.
exports.name: validateAgentCommand

exports.contract: agent-events reduce-agent --events emits ops-agent-events.status.v1 core status JSON from canonical event truth.
exports.name: reduceAgentCommand

exports.contract: Core backend status JSON generated from canonical event truth; it is compatible with agent-status-core-contract and is not a browser/CLI/MCP view contract.
exports.name: coreStatusJson

exports.contract: ops-agent-events.status.v1 must validate or normalize to agent-status-core.status.v1 without output drift unless an intentional-diff ledger approves it.
exports.name: agentStatusCoreCompatProducer

skillEntryBoundary.rule: Skills are entrypoints only. They may carry when-to-use and package selection guidance, but not normative policy, package contracts, implementation instructions, source/runtime payloads, handoff package bodies, review verdicts, or approval records.

definition.mission: Remove repeated human re-instruction cost for Codex/ChatGPT thread fleets while keeping implementation, review, governance, index, approval, merge, and policy-update authorities separated.
packageContents: schemas for mission ledger, worker result ingest, assignment, side issue, conflict, and projection records
definition.responsibility: Define the thin mission-control rail that fixes the human's mainline goal, records thread assignments, consumes worker.result.v1 JSONL as low-trust claims, parks side issues, and emits a non-authoritative next-action/status projection.

mustFailIf: worker.result.v1 self-report is treated as approval or verified fact
index emits approval, merge-ready, complete-approved, or canonical adoption
governance emits direct implementation assignment, merge approval, push, or policy rewrite
side issue captures the mainline without must-block reason code
stale checkpoint is accepted as current
missing or invalid worker JSON is handled by ad hoc human reminder instead of a record
projection hides conflicts by picking a single winner without evidence
the package creates duplicate canonical truth beside existing Board/JSONL authorities
message guide output is treated as assignment, send authorization, approval, merge-ready, or policy rewrite
ADR raw intent is copied into specs as a second source of truth instead of imported from adr
mustPass: package contract is discoverable from specs
purpose lineage through meta10 retained-earnings assumption is explicit
mission ledger records mainlineGoal, currentCheckpointId, assignments, side issues, and resumeRule
worker.result.v1 is consumed as claim input only
missing, invalid, unsupported-schema, and stale-checkpoint worker results are recordable without human scolding
index lane is snapshot-only and governance lane is report-only
side issue default is non-blocking
must-block reasons are explicit and evidence-bearing
projection exposes conflicts instead of collapsing them
message guide outputs are explicitly suggestion-only and require gate acceptance
ADR input relation completeness is present when ADR intent is cited
CLI use is optional for operators and not required for external workers

activationCriteria: worker.result.v1 schema and importer failure records are implementation-ready
mission ledger append-only identity and checkpoint stale detection are specified
thread lane authority ceilings are enforced by reducer/projection tests
index and governance lanes are represented as non-approving report lanes
side issue blocker reason codes and resume rule are represented in tests
projection distinguishes recordStatus from workStatus and never promotes claims to approval
message guide candidates require a separate authorized gate before send, assignment, approval, merge, or policy change
ADR raw intent, purpose lineage, destructive-case candidates, and promotion relations are available as Nix-readable input when this package cites ADR intent

adrIntentTraceabilityContract.degenPreventionRule: If specs loses the ADR relation input, the package is regression-prone and should surface an incomplete-trace blocker rather than silently accepting the degraded contract.
adrIntentTraceabilityContract.nonAuthorityRule: ADR relation input does not approve, merge, assign, send, rewrite policy, or mutate canonical; it only explains why the contract exists and what would break it.
adrIntentTraceabilityContract.relationRule: Specs intent must be traceable from a raw ADR record to promoted purpose lineage and destructive-test candidates before it is treated as complete for package contract review.

blockedWhen: threadLane is outside the allowed lane set
authorityCeiling is missing or exceeds lane authority
side issue claims blocksMainline without blockerReasonCode and unblockCondition
worker result lacks valid single-line JSON
claim lacks evidenceRef but is projected as verified

codexDelegationStateCeilingContract.purpose: Make Codex delegate more to ChatGPT while limiting Codex to explicit states and preserving local gates.

forbiddenBeforeActivation: building a general PM agent that assigns, approves, merges, pushes, sends, or rewrites policy
making worker.result.v1 a truth source instead of low-trust claim input
requiring workers to run a CLI as the primary compliance path
allowing index to approve, validate facts, declare merge-ready, or close work
allowing governance to issue implementation orders or policy rewrites inside a mainline task
treating side issues as blockers without explicit must-block reason and unblock condition
forbiddenPaths: duplicate canonical Board or JSONL store

blockedWhen: branch is stale against local bare SSOT
Project Source readback mismatches source-pack manifest
proposal batch has unresolved text or semantic conflict
gate logs missing or self-reported only

gateRecords.passRule: Final merge may be considered only when every required gate record has pass status and evidence hashes; ChatGPT claims are not pass evidence.

localCanonicalBranchHandoff.identityRule: Use projectId + proposalId + branchId + baseHash; never identify a proposal by branch name or zip filename alone.
localCanonicalBranchHandoff.staleRule: If canonicalHeadHash differs from current local bare SSOT or expiresAt has passed, return stale-branch and do not merge.

multiProposalMergeQueue.purpose: Queue multiple Project proposals for conflict detection, dependency ordering, and gate scheduling without granting approval authority.

localCanonicalBranchMergeQueueContract.purpose: Shift Codex from per-Project localization to local-canonical-branch merge gates that can receive multiple proposal-as-worktree candidates.

removedCodexBurden: repeat Project Source localization per proposal
repeat policy/context explanation per role thread

automaticPostcondition: dangerous action is stopped before any resume
violation or policy gap is recorded as mission.side-issue.v1
policy update is not started inside the worker lane
nextAction returns to the mainline when mustBlock is false
mustBlock requires explicit reason code and unblock condition
mainlineRailContract.resumeRule: After recording a non-blocking side issue, emit currentCheckpointId and nextAction for the original mainline before proposing policy work.

messageGuideBoundaryContract.sourceBoundaryRule: Guides must not copy raw Project Source, private transcript, or secret material into suggested messages; they may cite sourcePointer/evidenceRef instead.
messageGuideBoundaryContract.staleLearningRule: Past result recommendations must preserve age, source mission, outcome, and failure evidence. Old success patterns cannot override current lane authority or safety gates.

rawDefinition.mission: Remove repeated human re-instruction cost for Codex/ChatGPT thread fleets while keeping implementation, review, governance, index, approval, merge, and policy-update authorities separated.

missionLedgerContract.appendOnlyRule: Records are append-only. Corrections are mission.correction.v1 records that reference the wrong record; in-place mutation of prior records is forbidden.
missionLedgerContract.hashRule: Reducers SHOULD preserve recordId, prevRecordHash, payloadHash, and sourceRecordId when an implementation is available; absence of hashes is a residual risk, not proof of validity.

outputReviewGate.appOutputRule: Flake application outputs and slash-form application package outputs are forbidden. Package work must expose packages.<system> outputs only.
outputReviewGate.devShellOutputRule: devShells are local environment conveniences, not feat package work outputs.
outputReviewGate.exceptionRule: Only a specs-declared package-specific outputReviewGate exception may relax forbidden top-level outputs.
outputReviewGate.nixosModulesRule: If requiredOutputs contains nixosModules.*, generated inputs must mark module output review as required unless the flake exposes the module output.
outputReviewGate.packageOutputRule: officialOutput is the canonical Nix package output for the worker task.

packageContents: schemas for mission ledger, worker result ingest, assignment, side issue, conflict, and projection records
rawDefinition.plannedReason: The canonical contracts already contain worker-result, thread-FSM, history JSONL, and board projection pieces. This package only binds them into an explicit mainline rail and reducer contract; implementation remains blocked until schemas and gates are implemented in ops.

blockedWhen: submitter thread cannot read canonical source pack
review performed by unrelated thread without successor delegation
postMergeCanonicalReviewContract.closureRule: A proposal is not closed after canonical merge until proposal.postMergeCanonicalReview.v1 is expected-match or a mismatch has an accepted correction/reopen disposition. Emergency-deferred requires a named authorized override record and later review.
postMergeCanonicalReviewContract.purpose: Require the original worktree-submission ChatGPT thread to verify that post-merge canonical matches the proposal's expected intent.

requiredRecord.authority: claim-only; does not approve, merge, push, rewrite policy, or satisfy CI/Nix/test gates

reviewer.fallback: explicitly delegated successor thread with same proposalId and authority ceiling
forbidden: Codex-only closure without submitter review unless emergency override

projectSourceProposalRailContract.purpose: Lower Codex burden by moving stable context into a narrow Project Source source-pack and using proposal threads as the unit of conversation.

threadRule.explanation: threadLane/threadFunction/authorityCeiling remain worker.result fields. Do not create a permanent index/governance/impl/review thread fleet for every proposal unless a mission explicitly requires it.

projectionContract.conflictRule: Conflicting worker statuses are not collapsed into one truth. Projection emits overallStatus=conflicted and preserves byLane evidence.
projectionContract.openQuestionRule: openQuestions can block only when answerNeededForNextAction=true and the question is tied to the current checkpoint.

exports.contract: Append-only mission, checkpoint, assignment, side-issue, worker-result-ingest, correction, and projection records keyed by missionId.

purposeLineage.directPurpose: Stop losing the mainline task when a worker discovers policy gaps, violations, missing JSONL, or thread-state confusion.
purposeLineage.meta10Purpose: Contribute to retained earnings by increasing reliable throughput, lowering coordination and defect costs, and reducing avoidable interruption of value-producing work.
purposeLineage.meta1Purpose: Reduce repeated operator prompts to each Codex/ChatGPT thread by making the required worker-result line and resume checkpoint part of the standard handoff.
purposeLineage.meta2Purpose: Make thread fleet state inspectable: what is assigned, what is waiting, what is stale, what is conflicted, and what next action is safe.
purposeLineage.meta3Purpose: Turn worker prose into append-only, low-trust claims with evidence pointers instead of relying on memory or self-reported completion.
purposeLineage.meta4Purpose: Keep side issues from capturing the project by making them non-blocking unless they match explicit must-block safety reasons.
purposeLineage.meta5Purpose: Preserve role separation: index snapshots, governance reports, implementation works, reviews review, and only authorized localizer/merge flows mutate canonical.
purposeLineage.meta6Purpose: Decrease cycle time and rework by detecting missing, invalid, stale, unsupported, and conflicting worker records automatically.
purposeLineage.meta7Purpose: Improve review quality and auditability by requiring claim/evidence/checkpoint identity before status projections imply progress.
purposeLineage.meta8Purpose: Improve delivery throughput without weakening safety gates, by making resume-to-mainline the default postcondition after side-issue capture.
purposeLineage.meta9Purpose: Convert governance overhead into reusable operational infrastructure rather than recurring bespoke human coordination.

rawDefinition.responsibility: Define the thin mission-control rail that fixes the human's mainline goal, records thread assignments, consumes worker.result.v1 JSONL as low-trust claims, parks side issues, and emits a non-authoritative next-action/status projection.

threadLaneContract.governanceRule: Governance is report-only: it may emit policyFinding, risk, recommendation, and candidate-policy-change, but must not assign implementation, approve, merge, push, or rewrite policy inside the mainline mission.
threadLaneContract.indexRule: Index is snapshot-only: it may report observed/missing/invalid/stale/conflict/latestByTimestamp, but must not emit approved, valid-as-fact, merge-ready, or complete-approved.
threadLaneContract.laneFunctionRule: threadLane is the operational lane. threadFunction remains null for index/governance and remains one of impl-work, impl-review, merge-work, merge-review for existing role threads.

lanes.output: snapshot zip, records projection, missing/invalid/conflict report
policy finding, risk, recommendation, source-boundary report
merge candidate artifact and local gate evidence

workerResultContract.claimRule: Every worker claim is low-trust until backed by evidenceRef, artifact hash, gate log, review verdict, or sourceRecordId. Projection must show unverified claims separately.
workerResultContract.noCliRule: Workers are not required to run a mission-control CLI. The handoff requires one valid machine-readable JSONL worker.result.v1 line in the reply or returned artifact.

staleCheckpoint.nextAction: refresh checkpoint and retry from current mainline
staleCheckpoint.reason: mainlineCheckpointId is not current for missionId

definition.mission: Stabilize ChatGPT thread / Codex / CDP work without building a large coding agent.
requiredCommands: ops-thread-fsm check-ready --delivery MATERIALIZE_MANIFEST.json --impl-review impl-review.json --local-gate local-gate.json --merge-review merge-review.json --run-evidence-ledger merge-evidence-ledger.jsonl --run-report RUN_REPORT.md --json
definition.responsibility: Small FSM controller harness for ChatGPT/CDP work: state transitions, readback classification, retry reasons, prompt capsule, delivery verification, impl review, merge review, and merge-ready evidence checks.

mustFailIf: reimplements dependencies
streaming complete
done-only complete
merge-ready without delivery-verified
merge-ready without impl-review or merge-review
merge-ready without merge evidence ledger or RUN_REPORT projection
impl-review-pass appears only in prose but is accepted
merge-review-pass appears only in prose but is accepted
AGENTS.md long-form copy
ready-for-merge-review and merge-ready are conflated
unsafe merge/push/overwrite scope auto-continues
missing noMerge/noPush/noOverwrite is accepted
allowed-to-implement implies handoff, merge-ready, canonical merge, push, or overwrite
external output is accepted without send confirmation and readback
discussion completes with only one counterparty no-objections
discussion completes with stale proposalRevision
discussion completes while any counterparty reports UNRESOLVED_OBJECTIONS
handoff-created or planner-targets-ready is treated as success
worker.result.v1 self-report is treated as impl-review-pass, merge-review-pass, ready-for-merge-review, merge-ready, or canonical adoption
missing or invalid worker result JSON is handled only by human reminder instead of a typed record
side issue blocks the mainline without explicit blocker reason code and evidence
stale mainline checkpoint moves the mission backward
mustPass: packages.<system>.ops-thread-fsm
checks.<system>.ops-thread-fsm
streaming waits
done-only output-missing
impl-review reject retries
merge-review reject retries
merge-ready requires delivery, impl-review, local gate, merge-review, merge evidence ledger, and RUN_REPORT projection
impl-review-pass only passes impl-review when first non-empty line is exactly the token or structured verdict
merge-review-pass only passes merge-review when first non-empty line is exactly the token or structured verdict
generic review-pass/pass/passed/合格, negated token mentions, and cross-gate tokens do not pass review gates
dry-run writes false sends false
false blocker detected by readback evidence
complete pre-authorized non-destructive safe plan auto-continues
missing noMerge/noPush/noOverwrite fails closed as insufficient-plan
noMerge=false/noPush=false/noOverwrite=false escalates
allowed-to-implement does not imply worktree/artifact/review/merge/push/overwrite
ready-for-merge-review requires delivery, impl-review-pass, local gate evidence, merge evidence ledger, and RUN_REPORT projection
merge-ready additionally requires merge-review-pass
discussion-required tasks require same-revision no-objections from all required counterparties
stale proposal revision responses and missing counterparties do not complete discussion
unresolved objections keep discussion open or escalate to parent
facilitate-discussion requires worker-readable bootstrap context before producing thread controls
facilitate-discussion confirms only same-revision no-objections from both threads and otherwise returns response-required or revision-update-required
handoff-created and planner-targets-ready are continuation states, not terminal success
worker.result.v1 is classified as claim-only and never as approval or merge-ready
missing, invalid, unsupported-schema, and stale-checkpoint worker results fail closed into typed records
non-blocking side issues resume the current mainline checkpoint
blocker side issues require blockerReasonCode, unblockCondition, and evidence

blockedWhen: same-revision no-objections missing from any required counterparty
worker result is missing, invalid, unsupported, or stale and no retry/result record exists
side issue claims blocksMainline without blockerReasonCode, unblockCondition, and evidenceRef

guarantees: streaming waits
done retries
fail retries
delivery is required for ready
delivery requires manifest rows with path, bytes, sha256, fileIndex, and fileCount
impl-review-pass is the only impl-review pass token and must be the first non-empty line or structured verdict
merge-review-pass is the only merge-review pass token and must be the first non-empty line or structured verdict
generic review-pass/pass/合格, negated pass text, prose mentions, and cross-gate tokens do not pass review gates
impl-review and merge-review are required for ready
dry-run safe
false blockers are separated from real blockers by evidence/readback
safe auto-continue requires pre-authorized non-destructive scope and safety proofs
noMerge/noPush/noOverwrite missing or false fails closed or escalates
ready-for-merge-review is distinct from merge-ready
allowed-to-implement does not grant merge/push/overwrite/handoff permissions
discussion loop confirms only same-revision NO_UNRESOLVED_OBJECTIONS from all required counterparties
discussion loop returns response-required for missing or stale-revision counterparties
discussion loop returns objections-present for unresolved objections
discussion loop returns blocked-needs-parent for parent-decision objections
facilitate-discussion requires Project Source, policy, purpose lineage, review quality checks, and two thread bindings before producing controls
facilitate-discussion emits pointer-only controls for missing same-revision responses
facilitate-discussion converts accepted/objection markers to same-revision discussion classifications
handoff and merge continuation states are non-terminal
worker result recordStatus is separated from workStatus
stale checkpoint and authority ceiling violations fail closed
resume-mainline is emitted after non-blocking side issue capture

merge-evidence-ledger.purpose: Evaluate ready-for-merge-review and merge-ready from typed evidence rows while keeping RUN_REPORT.md as a generated projection.

implementationContract.completionRule: isStreaming:true and ChatGPT done prose are not complete; impl-review requires first-line explicit impl-review-pass verdict; merge-review requires first-line explicit merge-review-pass verdict; generic review-pass/pass/合格 and negated token mentions are insufficient; complete only on delivery + impl-review + local gate + merge-review + merge evidence ledger + RUN_REPORT projection.

discussionClassificationContract.markerRule: Marker fallback must avoid substring false positives such as NO_UNRESOLVED_OBJECTIONS matching UNRESOLVED_OBJECTIONS.

implementationContract.discussionCompletionRule: Discussion-required tasks complete only when every required counterparty returns explicit NO_UNRESOLVED_OBJECTIONS on the same proposalRevision. UNRESOLVED_OBJECTIONS, missing counterparty response, stale revision response, handoff-created, planner-targets-ready, readback, or Project Source visibility are not discussion completion.
implementationContract.discussionFacilitationRule: facilitate-discussion is controller-only. It requires discussionId, proposalRevision, Project Source proposal/entrypoint, policy snapshot or refs, purpose lineage depth 3..0, reviewQualityChecks, and at least two thread actor bindings. It emits pointer-only controls or a next revision requirement; it does not upload, send, readback, approve, localize, merge, push, or cleanup.

mainlineResumeContract.policyGapRule: Policy gaps are side issues unless they match mustBlockReasonCodes; policy rewriting is not started by the worker lane.
mainlineResumeContract.resumeStateRule: If no must-block reason is present, the controller returns resume-mainline with currentCheckpointId and nextAction from the mainline mission.

implementationContract.reviewPassRule: Gate-specific review pass is accepted only if the first non-empty line is exactly impl-review-pass or merge-review-pass for the matching gate, or an equally structured explicit verdict such as verdict: impl-review-pass, status: merge-review-pass, or result=merge-review-pass. A token appearing later in prose, in backticks, in a negation, with extra words on the same line, or in the wrong gate is not pass.
implementationContract.safeContinueCompletionRule: request-sent includes sleep 900 before delegated readback. Safe auto-continue requires complete pre-authorized non-destructive plan with base/upstream/head/worktree/branch/success/failure/gates/localBase/reportableEvidence/mergeEvidenceLedger and noMerge/noPush/noOverwrite true. Missing safety proof is insufficient-plan. Merge/push/overwrite/canonical merge, false noMerge/noPush/noOverwrite, or human judgment is escalation-needed. ready-for-merge-review is distinct from merge-ready.

safeContinuePlanContract.externalThreadRule: external-thread work is not requested without send confirmation and is not received without readback plus response evidence.
safeContinuePlanContract.falseBlockerRule: claims like not sent, thread not created, not in Project, or cannot connect require evidence; readback contradiction is false-blocker, not real-blocker.
safeContinuePlanContract.mergeReadyRule: merge-ready additionally requires explicit merge-review-pass recorded as review.verdict.v1 or equivalent gate-specific verdict evidence.
safeContinuePlanContract.permissionRule: allowed-to-implement never implies create-worktree, return-artifact, send-review, merge-ready, canonical merge, push, or overwrite.
safeContinuePlanContract.readyForMergeReviewRule: ready-for-merge-review requires delivery-verified, impl-review-pass, local gate evidence, merge evidence ledger, and RUN_REPORT projection; it is not merge-ready.

workerResultGateContract.authorityRule: index and governance lane results cannot pass implementation or merge gates; impl/merge work/review gates still require the existing gate-specific evidence.
workerResultGateContract.checkpointRule: mainlineCheckpointId must match the current mission checkpoint; stale checkpoints become worker-result-stale-checkpoint and do not move the mainline backward.
workerResultGateContract.claimOnlyRule: A valid worker.result.v1 never implies impl-review-pass, merge-review-pass, ready-for-merge-review, merge-ready, completion approval, localizer approval, merge, push, cleanup, or canonical adoption.
workerResultGateContract.missingInvalidRule: Missing or invalid JSONL becomes a typed record and structured-result retry, not an operator-written reminder.

knowledgeContract.discussionLoop: For discussion-required tasks, use discussionId, proposalRevision, noObjectionsRequiredFrom, and per-actor same-revision responses. Same-revision NO_UNRESOLVED_OBJECTIONS from all required counterparties confirms the discussion. Any UNRESOLVED_OBJECTIONS response keeps the loop open unless it requires parent decision. Handoff/readback states remain continuation states, not success.
knowledgeContract.gateFlow: source pack; send; wait with sleep 900; CDP readback; output classification; materialize via ops-artifact-materialize; delivery-verified requires BEGIN_B64_FILE materialize manifest rows with path, fileCount/fileIndex, bytes/bytesExpected, sha256/sha256Expected plus merge evidence ledger rows and readable RUN_REPORT projection; impl-review passes only on explicit impl-review-pass; merge local gate uses ops-runbook-checks; merge-review passes only on explicit merge-review-pass; generic review-pass/pass/合格 and negated pass prose fail closed.
knowledgeContract.ledger: Canonical ledger is ops-agent-events events.jsonl. Generated state.tsv/status.md are views. state.json is not canonical. Raw artifacts are not embedded.
knowledgeContract.loop: send; confirm success only; sleep 900; CDP readback; classify; materialize; delivery-verified; impl-review; merge local gate; merge-review; merge-ready. isStreaming:true and done prose are not complete.
knowledgeContract.promptCapsule: Prompt capsule carries goal, source archive, acceptance, phase/requestKind/state, required materializable artifact, merge evidence ledger, and RUN_REPORT projection. Footer forbids heartbeat/reminder/Codex app automation; requires sleep 900, CDP readback, strict streaming handling, delivery-verified materialize manifest rows, impl-review-pass or impl-review-reject, merge-review-pass or merge-review-reject, and merge-ready only after delivery, impl-review, local gate, merge-review, merge evidence ledger, and RUN_REPORT projection.
knowledgeContract.safeContinue: Plan evaluation separates real-blocker, false-blocker, insufficient-plan, plan-accepted, and escalation-needed. Pre-authorized non-destructive plans may continue without extra user/gen0 agreement only when noMerge/noPush/noOverwrite are true and required evidence is present. allowed-to-implement does not imply worktree creation, artifact return, review send, merge-ready, canonical merge, push, or overwrite.
knowledgeContract.summary: Small FSM controller harness. Delegates CDP to ops-cdp-core, materialize to ops-artifact-materialize, ledger to ops-agent-events, and local gates to ops-runbook-checks.

guaranteeCeiling.notActorSuccessProof: This governance package does not prove actor repair quality, patch correctness, approval, deploy, or rollback success.
guaranteeCeiling.notCompleteEvidenceRouter: This governance package does not by itself prove every Project Source item was correctly routed; merge review and issue evidence must verify that separately.
guaranteeCeiling.notRootCauseOracle: This governance package rejects hidden package-output state, but it does not prove that a detected incident has one true root cause.
guaranteeCeiling.notRuntimeDetector: This governance package is not a runtime error detector and does not observe production logs.

invariants.rule: Every active package id must resolve to its own packages/<id>/default.nix or grouped package default.nix.

Candidates, blockers, gaps, unresolved proposals, closure criteria, and work status remain in issue/evidence records. This is a policy/review invariant; the executable check only rejects the declared package-output hidden-state field shapes.
No active package-output graph/status/adoption/intake package may store candidate readiness or disposition state.
Not-ready work goes to issue/evidence records, not to a package-output graph package.
Package meaning must not depend on an idea/intake/adoption/ready/active phase chain.

Specs owns contracts, schemas, checks, validators, repo binding, return contracts, and specs-consumed golden fixtures; feat repos own runtime code, mutable state, deploy wiring, vendor integration, and non-specs-consumed examples. This is a review invariant, not fully proven by the field-name check.

Raw zips, mutable DB files, live Cloudflare/GitHub/VPS state, canonical checkouts, actor vendor binaries, deployment wiring, and raw log warehouses are not package outputs. This package checks the package-output hidden-state subset and leaves broad artifact classification to review/governance gates.

Project Source evidence, source zips, thread replies, upload/readback, and discussion files cannot become package truth merely by being mentioned. The executable check does not prove all evidence routing; merge review must verify evidence import remains issue/evidence only.

Heal-ourselves main localization is localize-ready only when issue evidence proves exact candidate destinations, current package-id resolution for create/keep/strengthen rows, issue/evidence records for deferred rows, prove-feat routing or blockers for feat candidates, and no persisted graph/adoption/intake package truth.
